meta.note: Break-glass fallback (?mock=1). The DEFAULT path is the live SSE stream from /api/migrate. This scripted run mirrors a real LAZARUS migration for stage safety.

events.text: Reading the entire module in one pass (1M-token context). This program accepts a numeric string, computes tax at a fixed rate, derives net pay, and DISPLAYs it. The fields use COMP-3 storage, but output is via COBOL numeric DISPLAY de-editing — I must mirror its formatting + rounding exactly.

24 src/payroll.cob
11:       01  WS-GROSS-PAY      PIC 9(7)V99 COMP-3.
13:       01  WS-TAX            PIC 9(7)V99 COMP-3.
14:       01  WS-NET            PIC 9(7)V99 COMP-3.
Before translating, recover the undocumented business logic this code encodes — the institutional knowledge that retired with its authors.

events.title: Free-form numeric input
events.plain: Gross pay arrives as a 12-character string and is parsed numerically (NUMVAL), tolerating leading spaces and missing leading zeros.

events.title: Flat 22.5% tax withholding
events.plain: Tax is 22.5% of gross pay, stored to two decimal places.

events.title: Net = gross − tax
events.plain: Net pay is gross minus the withheld tax, never recomputed from a rate.

events.title: DISPLAY de-editing + half-up rounding
events.plain: The tax uses COMPUTE ... ROUNDED, and net pay is shown via DISPLAY of a PIC 9(7)V99 field: COBOL ROUNDED is half-up (NOT Python's banker's rounding), and DISPLAY zero-pads to the full picture width ('0000775.00', not '775.0'). Get either wrong and the bytes diverge.

events.text: Writing idiomatic Python that mirrors the recovered rules. First-pass: standard rounding — I have no skill for COBOL's DISPLAY formatting + half-up rounding yet, so I'll use Python's default and let the oracle catch any drift.

events.title: write payroll.py
events.text:
def net_pay(raw: str) -> str:
    gross = float(raw.strip())
    tax = round(gross * 0.225, 2)        # default rounding (banker's)
    net = gross - tax
    return f"{net:010.2f}"

left.name: payroll.cob
left.code:
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. PAYROLL.
       DATA DIVISION.
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01  WS-GROSS-PAY  PIC 9(7)V99 COMP-3.
       01  WS-TAX-RATE   PIC V999 VALUE 0.225.
       01  WS-TAX        PIC 9(7)V99 COMP-3.
       01  WS-NET        PIC 9(7)V99 COMP-3.
       01  WS-IN         PIC X(12).
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
       MAIN-PARA.
           ACCEPT WS-IN.
           MOVE FUNCTION NUMVAL(WS-IN) TO WS-GROSS-PAY.
           COMPUTE WS-TAX ROUNDED =
               WS-GROSS-PAY * WS-TAX-RATE.
           COMPUTE WS-NET = WS-GROSS-PAY - WS-TAX.
           DISPLAY WS-NET.
           STOP RUN.

events.title: generate test_equivalence.py

cases.name: test_net_pay[00345.67]
cases.message: AssertionError: byte mismatch — banker's rounding rounded 77.775 up, COBOL ROUNDED is half-up

cases.name: test_net_pay[99999.99]
cases.message: AssertionError: byte mismatch — COBOL ROUNDED is half-up, not half-even

events.text: Both failures are off by exactly one cent on values whose tax ends in .5 at the third decimal. Root cause: Python's round() uses banker's rounding (half-to-even); COBOL COMPUTE ... ROUNDED is half-up. (COMP-3 storage is irrelevant — DISPLAY de-edits identically regardless.) This is an unknown idiom — I have no skill for COBOL's numeric DISPLAY + half-up rounding. I will FORGE one.

events.title: FORGE
events.text: Authoring .agents/skills/numeric-display-rounding/SKILL.md and committing it to the persistent environment.

events.reason: Unknown idiom: COBOL numeric DISPLAY de-editing + COMPUTE ROUNDED (half-up).

additions: # SKILL: COBOL numeric DISPLAY format + ROUND-HALF-UP
When COBOL uses `COMPUTE ... ROUNDED` and DISPLAYs a numeric
`PIC 9(n)V99` field, it rounds **half-up** at the field scale
(NOT Python's banker's rounding) and zero-pads to the full
Use Decimal + ROUND_HALF_UP, then format to the picture width:
# DISPLAY a 9(7)V99 field as zero-padded 10.2f, e.g. f"{net:010.2f}"
Apply to every numeric field DISPLAYed after COMPUTE ... ROUNDED.
git.commit: forge: add numeric DISPLAY + half-up rounding skill

events.title: Commit the forged skill
events.text: git add .agents/skills/numeric-display-rounding/SKILL.md && git commit -m 'forge: add numeric DISPLAY + half-up rounding skill'

events.text: Re-read the forged skill from the reused environment. Rewriting net_pay to use Decimal half-up rounding at the field scale, formatted to the DISPLAY picture width.

events.title: rewrite payroll.py
events.text:
from decimal import Decimal, ROUND_HALF_UP
CENT = Decimal('0.01')
def net_pay(raw: str) -> str:
    gross = Decimal(raw.strip())
    tax = (gross * Decimal('0.225')).quantize(CENT, ROUND_HALF_UP)
    net = gross - tax
    return f"{net:010.2f}"

       01  WS-TAX        PIC 9(7)V99 COMP-3.
       01  WS-NET        PIC 9(7)V99 COMP-3.

       PROCEDURE DIVISION.

right.name: payroll.py
right.code:
from decimal import Decimal, ROUND_HALF_UP
CENT = Decimal('0.01')

def net_pay(raw: str) -> str:
    gross = Decimal(raw.strip())
    tax = (gross * Decimal('0.225')).quantize(
        CENT, ROUND_HALF_UP)          # COBOL half-up
    net = gross - tax
    return f"{net:010.2f}"          # DISPLAY zero-pad to PIC width

events.title: Differential oracle: MATCH
events.text: Python output == real GnuCOBOL output across all 3 inputs. Equivalence is falsifiable — and not falsified.

events.name: payroll.py
events.content:
"""payroll.py — migrated from payroll.cob by LAZARUS.

Proven byte-for-byte equivalent to the original GnuCOBOL output across
the golden input battery. COBOL numeric DISPLAY de-editing + COMPUTE
ROUNDED (half-up) handled via the forged numeric-display-rounding skill.
"""
from decimal import Decimal, ROUND_HALF_UP

CENT = Decimal('0.01')
TAX_RATE = Decimal('0.225')


def net_pay(raw: str) -> str:
    """Compute net pay from a free-form gross-pay string.

    Mirrors PAYROLL.COB: flat 22.5% tax withheld with COBOL ROUNDED
    (half-up) semantics, net = gross - tax, formatted as the original
    zero-padded PIC 9(7)V99 DISPLAY field.
    """
    gross = Decimal(raw.strip())
    tax = (gross * TAX_RATE).quantize(CENT, rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP)
    net = gross - tax
    return f"{net:010.2f}"


if __name__ == '__main__':
    import sys
    print(net_pay(sys.stdin.readline()))

events.summary: Byte-for-byte equivalent to the mainframe across 3 inputs in 2 iterations.
